authors_text: Yushun Tang,Ce Zhang,Heng Xu,Shuoshuo Chen,Jie Cheng,Luziwei Leng,Qinghai Guo,Zhihai He
title: Neuro-Modulated Hebbian Learning for Fully Test-Time Adaptation
title_zh: 用于完全测试时间适应的神经调节赫布学习
abstract: Fully test-time adaptation aims to adapt the network model based on sequential analysis of input samples during the inference stage to address the cross-domain performance degradation problem of deep neural networks. We take inspiration from the biological plausibility learning where the neuron responses are tuned based on a local synapse-change procedure and activated by competitive lateral inhibition rules. Based on these feed-forward learning rules, we design a soft Hebbian learning process which provides an unsupervised and effective mechanism for online adaptation. We observe that the performance of this feed-forward Hebbian learning for fully test-time adaptation can be significantly improved by incorporating a feedback neuro-modulation layer. It is able to fine-tune the neuron responses based on the external feedback generated by the error back-propagation from the top inference layers. This leads to our proposed neuro-modulated Hebbian learning (NHL) method for fully test-time adaptation. With the unsupervised feed-forward soft Hebbian learning being combined with a learned neuro-modulator to capture feedback from external responses, the source model can be effectively adapted during the testing process. Experimental results on benchmark datasets demonstrate that our proposed method can significantly improve the adaptation performance of network models and outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods.
summary: Fully test-time adaptation aims to adapt the network model based on sequential analysis of input samples during the inference stage to address the cross-domain performance degradation problem of deep neural networks. We take inspiration from the biological plausibility learning where the neuron responses are tuned based on a local synapse-change procedure and activated by competitive lateral inhibition rules. Based on these feed-forward learning rules, we design a soft Hebbian learning process which provides an unsupervised and effective mechanism for online adaptation. We observe that the performance of this feed-forward Hebbian learning for fully test-time adaptation can be significantly improved by incorporating a feedback neuro-modulation layer. It is able to fine-tune the neuron responses based on the external feedback generated by the error back-propagation from the top inference layers. This leads to our proposed neuro-modulated Hebbian learning (NHL) method for fully test-time adaptation. With the unsupervised feed-forward soft Hebbian learning being combined with a learned neuro-modulator to capture feedback from external responses, the source model can be effectively adapted during the testing process. Experimental results on benchmark datasets demonstrate that our proposed method can significantly improve the adaptation performance of network models and outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods.
abstract_zh: 完全测试时自适应旨在在推理阶段基于输入样本的顺序分析来调整网络模型，以解决深度神经网络的跨域性能下降问题。我们从生物合理性学习中获得灵感，其中神经元反应根据局部突触变化过程进行调整，并通过竞争性侧抑制规则激活。基于这些前馈学习规则，我们设计了一个软赫布学习过程，为在线适应提供了无监督且有效的机制。我们观察到，通过合并反馈神经调制层，可以显着提高这种用于完全测试时间适应的前馈赫布学习的性能。它能够根据来自顶部推理层的误差反向传播生成的外部反馈来微调神经元响应。这导致了我们提出的神经调节赫布学习（NHL）方法，用于完全测试时间适应。通过将无监督前馈软赫布学习与学习的神经调制器相结合以捕获来自外部响应的反馈，可以在测试过程中有效地调整源模型。基准数据集上的实验结果表明，我们提出的方法可以显着提高网络模型的适应性能，并且优于现有的最先进方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于神经调节赫布学习的完全测试时自适应方法，解决深度神经网络跨域性能下降问题。
AI.motivation: 深度神经网络在跨域场景下性能下降，传统方法依赖标注数据，而生物启发的赫布学习机制可实现无监督自适应，但需结合反馈调节以提升效果。
AI.method: 设计软赫布学习机制结合无监督前馈学习与反馈神经调节层，利用误差反向传播生成的外部反馈动态调整个体神经元响应。
AI.result: 在基准数据集上显著优于现有最先进方法，验证了神经调节赫布学习在测试时自适应中的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 通过融合生物可塑性学习规则与反馈调节机制，提出的NHL方法实现了高效无监督的测试时模型自适应，为跨域场景提供新解决方案。

search_text: Neuro-Modulated Hebbian Learning for Fully Test-Time Adaptation 用于完全测试时间适应的神经调节赫布学习 Fully test-time adaptation aims to adapt the network model based on sequential analysis of input samples during the inference stage to address the cross-domain performance degradation problem of deep neural networks. We take inspiration from the biological plausibility learning where the neuron responses are tuned based on a local synapse-change procedure and activated by competitive lateral inhibition rules. Based on these feed-forward learning rules, we design a soft Hebbian learning process which provides an unsupervised and effective mechanism for online adaptation. We observe that the performance of this feed-forward Hebbian learning for fully test-time adaptation can be significantly improved by incorporating a feedback neuro-modulation layer. It is able to fine-tune the neuron responses based on the external feedback generated by the error back-propagation from the top inference layers. This leads to our proposed neuro-modulated Hebbian learning (NHL) method for fully test-time adaptation. With the unsupervised feed-forward soft Hebbian learning being combined with a learned neuro-modulator to capture feedback from external responses, the source model can be effectively adapted during the testing process. Experimental results on benchmark datasets demonstrate that our proposed method can significantly improve the adaptation performance of network models and outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods. 完全测试时自适应旨在在推理阶段基于输入样本的顺序分析来调整网络模型，以解决深度神经网络的跨域性能下降问题。我们从生物合理性学习中获得灵感，其中神经元反应根据局部突触变化过程进行调整，并通过竞争性侧抑制规则激活。基于这些前馈学习规则，我们设计了一个软赫布学习过程，为在线适应提供了无监督且有效的机制。我们观察到，通过合并反馈神经调制层，可以显着提高这种用于完全测试时间适应的前馈赫布学习的性能。它能够根据来自顶部推理层的误差反向传播生成的外部反馈来微调神经元响应。这导致了我们提出的神经调节赫布学习（NHL）方法，用于完全测试时间适应。通过将无监督前馈软赫布学习与学习的神经调制器相结合以捕获来自外部响应的反馈，可以在测试过程中有效地调整源模型。基准数据集上的实验结果表明，我们提出的方法可以显着提高网络模型的适应性能，并且优于现有的最先进方法。 本文提出一种基于神经调节赫布学习的完全测试时自适应方法，解决深度神经网络跨域性能下降问题。 Yushun Tang,Ce Zhang,Heng Xu,Shuoshuo Chen,Jie Cheng,Luziwei Leng,Qinghai Guo,Zhihai He cs.CV

authors_text: Woojun Kim,Youngchul Sung
title: Parameter Sharing with Network Pruning for Scalable Multi-Agent Deep Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: 通过网络修剪进行参数共享，实现可扩展的多智能体深度强化学习
abstract: Handling the problem of scalability is one of the essential issues for multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) algorithms to be applied to real-world problems typically involving massively many agents. For this, parameter sharing across multiple agents has widely been used since it reduces the training time by decreasing the number of parameters and increasing the sample efficiency. However, using the same parameters across agents limits the representational capacity of the joint policy and consequently, the performance can be degraded in multi-agent tasks that require different behaviors for different agents. In this paper, we propose a simple method that adopts structured pruning for a deep neural network to increase the representational capacity of the joint policy without introducing additional parameters. We evaluate the proposed method on several benchmark tasks, and numerical results show that the proposed method significantly outperforms other parameter-sharing methods.
summary: Handling the problem of scalability is one of the essential issues for multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) algorithms to be applied to real-world problems typically involving massively many agents. For this, parameter sharing across multiple agents has widely been used since it reduces the training time by decreasing the number of parameters and increasing the sample efficiency. However, using the same parameters across agents limits the representational capacity of the joint policy and consequently, the performance can be degraded in multi-agent tasks that require different behaviors for different agents. In this paper, we propose a simple method that adopts structured pruning for a deep neural network to increase the representational capacity of the joint policy without introducing additional parameters. We evaluate the proposed method on several benchmark tasks, and numerical results show that the proposed method significantly outperforms other parameter-sharing methods.
abstract_zh: 处理可扩展性问题是将多智能体强化学习（MARL）算法应用于通常涉及大量智能体的现实问题的基本问题之一。为此，跨多个代理的参数共享已被广泛使用，因为它通过减少参数数量和提高样本效率来减少训练时间。然而，跨代理使用相同的参数限制了联合策略的表示能力，因此，在需要不同代理不同行为的多代理任务中，性能可能会下降。在本文中，我们提出了一种简单的方法，采用深度神经网络的结构化剪枝来增加联合策略的表示能力，而无需引入额外的参数。我们在几个基准任务上评估了所提出的方法，数值结果表明所提出的方法显着优于其他参数共享方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合参数共享与网络剪枝的方法，解决多智能体深度强化学习中的可扩展性问题。
AI.motivation: 参数共享虽能提升训练效率，但限制了多智能体联合策略的表达能力，导致复杂任务性能下降。
AI.method: 采用结构化剪枝技术，在保持参数共享的同时增强神经网络的表示能力。
AI.result: 在多个基准任务中，该方法显著优于传统参数共享方法，验证了有效性。
AI.conclusion: 所提方法通过网络剪枝平衡了参数共享的效率与策略表达能力，为可扩展MARL提供了新思路。

authors_text: Yusuke Sakurai,Tomoya Kamimura,Yuki Sakamoto,Shohei Nishii,Kodai Sato,Yuta Fujiwara,Akihito Sano
title: Bipedal Robot Running: Human-like Actuation Timing Using Fast and Slow Adaptations
title_zh: 双足机器人运行：使用快速和慢速适应的类人驱动时序
comment: 17 pages, 13 figures, accepted to Advanced Robotics
comments: 17 pages, 13 figures, accepted to Advanced Robotics
abstract: We have been developing human-sized biped robots based on passive dynamic mechanisms. In human locomotion, the muscles activate at the same rate relative to the gait cycle during running. To achieve adaptive running for robots, such characteristics should be reproduced to yield the desired effect, In this study, we designed a central pattern generator (CPG) involving fast and slow adaptation to achieve human-like running using a simple spring-mass model and our developed bipedal robot, which is equipped with actuators that imitate the human musculoskeletal system. Our results demonstrate that the CPG-based controller with fast and slow adaptations, and a adjustable actuator control timing can reproduce human-like running. The results suggest that the CPG contributes to the adjustment of the muscle activation timing in human running.
summary: We have been developing human-sized biped robots based on passive dynamic mechanisms. In human locomotion, the muscles activate at the same rate relative to the gait cycle during running. To achieve adaptive running for robots, such characteristics should be reproduced to yield the desired effect, In this study, we designed a central pattern generator (CPG) involving fast and slow adaptation to achieve human-like running using a simple spring-mass model and our developed bipedal robot, which is equipped with actuators that imitate the human musculoskeletal system. Our results demonstrate that the CPG-based controller with fast and slow adaptations, and a adjustable actuator control timing can reproduce human-like running. The results suggest that the CPG contributes to the adjustment of the muscle activation timing in human running.
abstract_zh: 我们一直在开发基于被动动态机制的人类大小的双足机器人。在人类运动中，肌肉以与跑步期间步态周期相同的速率激活。为了实现机器人的自适应运行，应该重现这些特性以产生预期的效果。在这项研究中，我们设计了一种中央模式生成器（CPG），涉及快速和慢速适应，以使用简单的弹簧质量模型和我们开发的双足机器人来实现类人运行，该双足机器人配备了模仿人类肌肉骨骼系统的执行器。我们的结果表明，基于 CPG 的控制器具有快速和慢速自适应功能，以及可调节执行器控制时序，可以重现类人跑步。结果表明，CPG 有助于调整人类跑步中的肌肉激活时间。

AI.tldr: 本文设计了一种结合快速和慢速适应的中央模式生成器（CPG）控制器，通过模仿人类肌肉激活机制，使双足机器人实现类人跑步动作。
AI.motivation: 人类跑步时肌肉激活速率与步态周期同步，为使机器人实现自适应跑步，需模仿该特性以提高运动效率和稳定性。
AI.method: 基于弹簧-质量模型和仿生执行器，开发包含快速/慢速适应机制的CPG控制器，通过调整执行器控制时序实现类人运动控制。
AI.result: 实验表明CPG控制器可使机器人重现人类跑步的肌肉激活时序特征，验证了该方法在运动控制中的有效性。
AI.conclusion: CPG机制能有效调节类人跑步的肌肉激活时序，为双足机器人自适应运动控制提供了新思路。

Our results demonstrate that the CPG-based controller with fast and slow adaptations, and a adjustable actuator control timing can reproduce human-like running. The results suggest that the CPG contributes to the adjustment of the muscle activation timing in human running. 我们一直在开发基于被动动态机制的人类大小的双足机器人。在人类运动中，肌肉以与跑步期间步态周期相同的速率激活。为了实现机器人的自适应运行，应该重现这些特性以产生预期的效果。在这项研究中，我们设计了一种中央模式生成器（CPG），涉及快速和慢速适应，以使用简单的弹簧质量模型和我们开发的双足机器人来实现类人运行，该双足机器人配备了模仿人类肌肉骨骼系统的执行器。我们的结果表明，基于 CPG 的控制器具有快速和慢速自适应功能，以及可调节执行器控制时序，可以重现类人跑步。结果表明，CPG 有助于调整人类跑步中的肌肉激活时间。 本文设计了一种结合快速和慢速适应的中央模式生成器（CPG）控制器，通过模仿人类肌肉激活机制，使双足机器人实现类人跑步动作。 Yusuke Sakurai,Tomoya Kamimura,Yuki Sakamoto,Shohei Nishii,Kodai Sato,Yuta Fujiwara,Akihito Sano cs.RO,eess.SY

authors_text: Austin Stone,Ted Xiao,Yao Lu,Keerthana Gopalakrishnan,Kuang-Huei Lee,Quan Vuong,Paul Wohlhart,Sean Kirmani,Brianna Zitkovich,Fei Xia,Chelsea Finn,Karol Hausman
title: Open-World Object Manipulation using Pre-trained Vision-Language Models
title_zh: 使用预先训练的视觉语言模型进行开放世界对象操作
comment: Accepted at the 7th Conference on Robot Learning (CoRL 2023)
comments: Accepted at the 7th Conference on Robot Learning (CoRL 2023)
abstract: For robots to follow instructions from people, they must be able to connect the rich semantic information in human vocabulary, e.g. "can you get me the pink stuffed whale?" to their sensory observations and actions. This brings up a notably difficult challenge for robots: while robot learning approaches allow robots to learn many different behaviors from first-hand experience, it is impractical for robots to have first-hand experiences that span all of this semantic information. We would like a robot's policy to be able to perceive and pick up the pink stuffed whale, even if it has never seen any data interacting with a stuffed whale before. Fortunately, static data on the internet has vast semantic information, and this information is captured in pre-trained vision-language models. In this paper, we study whether we can interface robot policies with these pre-trained models, with the aim of allowing robots to complete instructions involving object categories that the robot has never seen first-hand. We develop a simple approach, which we call Manipulation of Open-World Objects (MOO), which leverages a pre-trained vision-language model to extract object-identifying information from the language command and image, and conditions the robot policy on the current image, the instruction, and the extracted object information. In a variety of experiments on a real mobile manipulator, we find that MOO generalizes zero-shot to a wide range of novel object categories and environments. In addition, we show how MOO generalizes to other, non-language-based input modalities to specify the object of interest such as finger pointing, and how it can be further extended to enable open-world navigation and manipulation. The project's website and evaluation videos can be found at https://robot-moo.github.io/

In a variety of experiments on a real mobile manipulator, we find that MOO generalizes zero-shot to a wide range of novel object categories and environments. The project's website and evaluation videos can be found at https://robot-moo.github.io/
abstract_zh: 为了让机器人遵循人类的指令，它们必须能够连接人类词汇中丰富的语义信息，例如“ “你能给我粉色的鲸鱼标本吗？”他们的感官观察和行动。这给机器人带来了一个非常困难的挑战：虽然机器人学习方法允许机器人从第一手经验中学习许多不同的行为，但机器人拥有涵盖所有这些语义信息的第一手经验是不切实际的。我们希望机器人的策略能够感知并拾取粉红色的鲸鱼标本，即使它以前从未见过与鲸鱼标本交互的任何数据。幸运的是，互联网上的静态数据具有大量语义信息，并且这些信息是在预先训练的视觉语言模型中捕获的。在本文中，我们研究是否可以将机器人策略与这些预先训练的模型相结合，目的是让机器人完成涉及机器人从未亲眼见过的物体类别的指令。我们开发了一种简单的方法，称为开放世界对象的操纵（MOO），它利用预先训练的视觉语言模型从语言命令和图像中提取对象识别信息，并根据当前图像、指令和提取的对象信息来调节机器人策略。在真实移动机械臂上的各种实验中，我们发现 MOO 将零样本推广到各种新颖的对象类别和环境。此外，我们还展示了 MOO 如何推广到其他非基于语言的输入方式来指定感兴趣的对象（例如手指指向），以及如何进一步扩展它以实现开放世界的导航和操作。该项目的网站和评估视频可以在 https://robot-moo.github.io/ 找到

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种利用预训练视觉-语言模型实现机器人对未见过物体的开放世界操作的方法MOO。
AI.motivation: 机器人需理解人类复杂指令中的语义信息并执行操作，但难以通过有限的亲身经验覆盖所有可能的物体类别，需借助互联网上的静态语义数据。
AI.method: 开发MOO方法，通过预训练视觉-语言模型从指令和图像中提取物体特征信息，并将该信息与当前图像和指令结合，条件化控制机器人策略。
AI.result: 在真实移动机械臂上的实验表明，MOO可零样本泛化到多种新物体和环境，并支持指尖指向等非语言模态输入，可扩展至开放世界导航。
AI.conclusion: MOO方法有效结合预训练模型与机器人策略，为开放世界物体操作提供了可行方案，展示了实际应用潜力。

authors_text: Siyuan Yan,Jing Zhang,Nick Barnes
title: Transmission-Guided Bayesian Generative Model for Smoke Segmentation
title_zh: 用于烟雾分割的传输引导贝叶斯生成模型
abstract: Smoke segmentation is essential to precisely localize wildfire so that it can be extinguished in an early phase. Although deep neural networks have achieved promising results on image segmentation tasks, they are prone to be overconfident for smoke segmentation due to its non-rigid shape and transparent appearance. This is caused by both knowledge level uncertainty due to limited training data for accurate smoke segmentation and labeling level uncertainty representing the difficulty in labeling ground-truth. To effectively model the two types of uncertainty, we introduce a Bayesian generative model to simultaneously estimate the posterior distribution of model parameters and its predictions. Further, smoke images suffer from low contrast and ambiguity, inspired by physics-based image dehazing methods, we design a transmission-guided local coherence loss to guide the network to learn pair-wise relationships based on pixel distance and the transmission feature. To promote the development of this field, we also contribute a high-quality smoke segmentation dataset, SMOKE5K, consisting of 1,400 real and 4,000 synthetic images with pixel-wise annotation. Experimental results on benchmark testing datasets illustrate that our model achieves both accurate predictions and reliable uncertainty maps representing model ignorance about its prediction. Our code and dataset are publicly available at: https://github.com/redlessme/Transmission-BVM.
summary: Smoke segmentation is essential to precisely localize wildfire so that it can be extinguished in an early phase. Although deep neural networks have achieved promising results on image segmentation tasks, they are prone to be overconfident for smoke segmentation due to its non-rigid shape and transparent appearance. This is caused by both knowledge level uncertainty due to limited training data for accurate smoke segmentation and labeling level uncertainty representing the difficulty in labeling ground-truth. To effectively model the two types of uncertainty, we introduce a Bayesian generative model to simultaneously estimate the posterior distribution of model parameters and its predictions. Further, smoke images suffer from low contrast and ambiguity, inspired by physics-based image dehazing methods, we design a transmission-guided local coherence loss to guide the network to learn pair-wise relationships based on pixel distance and the transmission feature. To promote the development of this field, we also contribute a high-quality smoke segmentation dataset, SMOKE5K, consisting of 1,400 real and 4,000 synthetic images with pixel-wise annotation. Experimental results on benchmark testing datasets illustrate that our model achieves both accurate predictions and reliable uncertainty maps representing model ignorance about its prediction. Our code and dataset are publicly available at: https://github.com/redlessme/Transmission-BVM.
abstract_zh: 烟雾分割对于精确定位野火至关重要，以便尽早扑灭野火。尽管深度神经网络在图像分割任务上取得了可喜的成果，但由于烟雾的非刚性形状和透明外观，它们很容易对烟雾分割过于自信。这是由于精确烟雾分割的训练数据有限而造成的知识水平不确定性和代表标记地面实况的难度的标签水平不确定性造成的。为了有效地建模这两类不确定性，我们引入了贝叶斯生成模型来同时估计模型参数的后验分布及其预测。此外，烟雾图像对比度低且模糊，受基于物理的图像去雾方法的启发，我们设计了一种传输引导的局部相干损失，以引导网络学习基于像素距离和传输特征的成对关系。为了促进这一领域的发展，我们还贡献了一个高质量的烟雾分割数据集 SMOKE5K，该数据集由 1,400 张真实图像和 4,000 张带有像素级注释的合成图像组成。基准测试数据集的实验结果表明，我们的模型实现了准确的预测和可靠的不确定性图（代表模型对其预测的无知）。我们的代码和数据集可在以下网址公开获取：https://github.com/redlessme/Transmission-BVM。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于贝叶斯生成模型的烟雾分割方法，通过建模不确定性提升分割精度。
AI.motivation: 现有深度网络在烟雾分割中因烟雾非刚性形态和透明特性易产生过拟合，且受限于数据量不足和标注困难导致的不确定性。
AI.method: 引入贝叶斯生成模型联合估计参数后验分布与预测不确定性，并设计传输引导的局部一致性损失以学习像素间关系，同时构建包含1,400真实图像和4,000合成图像的SMOKE5K数据集。
AI.result: 在基准数据集上实现准确分割与可靠不确定性映射，代码和数据集已开源。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效建模了烟雾分割中的双重不确定性，为领域发展提供了新思路和高质量数据支持。

Our code and dataset are publicly available at: https://github.com/redlessme/Transmission-BVM. 烟雾分割对于精确定位野火至关重要，以便尽早扑灭野火。尽管深度神经网络在图像分割任务上取得了可喜的成果，但由于烟雾的非刚性形状和透明外观，它们很容易对烟雾分割过于自信。这是由于精确烟雾分割的训练数据有限而造成的知识水平不确定性和代表标记地面实况的难度的标签水平不确定性造成的。为了有效地建模这两类不确定性，我们引入了贝叶斯生成模型来同时估计模型参数的后验分布及其预测。此外，烟雾图像对比度低且模糊，受基于物理的图像去雾方法的启发，我们设计了一种传输引导的局部相干损失，以引导网络学习基于像素距离和传输特征的成对关系。为了促进这一领域的发展，我们还贡献了一个高质量的烟雾分割数据集 SMOKE5K，该数据集由 1,400 张真实图像和 4,000 张带有像素级注释的合成图像组成。基准测试数据集的实验结果表明，我们的模型实现了准确的预测和可靠的不确定性图（代表模型对其预测的无知）。我们的代码和数据集可在以下网址公开获取：https://github.com/redlessme/Transmission-BVM。 本文提出一种基于贝叶斯生成模型的烟雾分割方法，通过建模不确定性提升分割精度。 Siyuan Yan,Jing Zhang,Nick Barnes cs.CV

authors_text: Peter Barnett,Rachel Freedman,Justin Svegliato,Stuart Russell
title: Active Reward Learning from Multiple Teachers
title_zh: 多位老师的积极奖励学习
abstract: Reward learning algorithms utilize human feedback to infer a reward function, which is then used to train an AI system. This human feedback is often a preference comparison, in which the human teacher compares several samples of AI behavior and chooses which they believe best accomplishes the objective. While reward learning typically assumes that all feedback comes from a single teacher, in practice these systems often query multiple teachers to gather sufficient training data. In this paper, we investigate this disparity, and find that algorithmic evaluation of these different sources of feedback facilitates more accurate and efficient reward learning. We formally analyze the value of information (VOI) when reward learning from teachers with varying levels of rationality, and define and evaluate an algorithm that utilizes this VOI to actively select teachers to query for feedback. Surprisingly, we find that it is often more informative to query comparatively irrational teachers. By formalizing this problem and deriving an analytical solution, we hope to facilitate improvement in reward learning approaches to aligning AI behavior with human values.
summary: Reward learning algorithms utilize human feedback to infer a reward function, which is then used to train an AI system. This human feedback is often a preference comparison, in which the human teacher compares several samples of AI behavior and chooses which they believe best accomplishes the objective. While reward learning typically assumes that all feedback comes from a single teacher, in practice these systems often query multiple teachers to gather sufficient training data. In this paper, we investigate this disparity, and find that algorithmic evaluation of these different sources of feedback facilitates more accurate and efficient reward learning. We formally analyze the value of information (VOI) when reward learning from teachers with varying levels of rationality, and define and evaluate an algorithm that utilizes this VOI to actively select teachers to query for feedback. Surprisingly, we find that it is often more informative to query comparatively irrational teachers. By formalizing this problem and deriving an analytical solution, we hope to facilitate improvement in reward learning approaches to aligning AI behavior with human values.
abstract_zh: 奖励学习算法利用人类反馈来推断奖励函数，然后用于训练人工智能系统。这种人类反馈通常是一种偏好比较，其中人类教师比较人工智能行为的几个样本，并选择他们认为最能实现目标的行为。虽然奖励学习通常假设所有反馈都来自单个教师，但在实践中，这些系统经常查询多个教师以收集足够的训练数据。在本文中，我们研究了这种差异，发现对这些不同反馈源的算法评估有助于更准确、更高效的奖励学习。我们正式分析了奖励不同理性水平的教师学习时的信息价值（VOI），并定义和评估了一种算法，利用该 VOI 主动选择教师来查询反馈。令人惊讶的是，我们发现询问相对不理性的老师往往会提供更多信息。通过形式化这个问题并得出分析解决方案，我们希望促进奖励学习方法的改进，使人工智能行为与人类价值观保持一致。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种通过主动选择多个教师反馈来优化奖励学习的新方法，以提高AI系统与人类价值观对齐的效率和准确性。
AI.motivation: 传统奖励学习通常假设单一教师反馈，但实际应用中多教师反馈更常见。如何有效整合不同教师（尤其是理性程度差异）的反馈是关键挑战。
AI.method: 通过形式化信息价值（VOI）理论，设计算法主动选择具有最高信息价值的教师进行查询，特别关注非理性教师可能提供的独特信息。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法显著提升奖励学习的准确性和效率，且发现非理性教师的反馈在特定场景下比理性教师更具信息价值。
AI.conclusion: 形式化分析教师反馈的VOI并主动选择查询对象，可有效改进多教师环境下的奖励学习，推动AI行为与人类价值观的对齐。

authors_text: Chengnan Shentu,Enxu Li,Chaojun Chen,Puspita Triana Dewi,David B. Lindell,Jessica Burgner-Kahrs
title: MoSS: Monocular Shape Sensing for Continuum Robots
title_zh: MoSS：连续体机器人的单目形状传感
abstract: Continuum robots are promising candidates for interactive tasks in medical and industrial applications due to their unique shape, compliance, and miniaturization capability. Accurate and real-time shape sensing is essential for such tasks yet remains a challenge. Embedded shape sensing has high hardware complexity and cost, while vision-based methods require stereo setup and struggle to achieve real-time performance. This paper proposes the first eye-to-hand monocular approach to continuum robot shape sensing. Utilizing a deep encoder-decoder network, our method, MoSSNet, eliminates the computation cost of stereo matching and reduces requirements on sensing hardware. In particular, MoSSNet comprises an encoder and three parallel decoders to uncover spatial, length, and contour information from a single RGB image, and then obtains the 3D shape through curve fitting. A two-segment tendon-driven continuum robot is used for data collection and testing, demonstrating accurate (mean shape error of 0.91 mm, or 0.36% of robot length) and real-time (70 fps) shape sensing on real-world data. Additionally, the method is optimized end-to-end and does not require fiducial markers, manual segmentation, or camera calibration. Code and datasets will be made available at https://github.com/ContinuumRoboticsLab/MoSSNet.
summary: Continuum robots are promising candidates for interactive tasks in medical and industrial applications due to their unique shape, compliance, and miniaturization capability. Accurate and real-time shape sensing is essential for such tasks yet remains a challenge. Embedded shape sensing has high hardware complexity and cost, while vision-based methods require stereo setup and struggle to achieve real-time performance. This paper proposes the first eye-to-hand monocular approach to continuum robot shape sensing. Utilizing a deep encoder-decoder network, our method, MoSSNet, eliminates the computation cost of stereo matching and reduces requirements on sensing hardware. In particular, MoSSNet comprises an encoder and three parallel decoders to uncover spatial, length, and contour information from a single RGB image, and then obtains the 3D shape through curve fitting. A two-segment tendon-driven continuum robot is used for data collection and testing, demonstrating accurate (mean shape error of 0.91 mm, or 0.36% of robot length) and real-time (70 fps) shape sensing on real-world data. Additionally, the method is optimized end-to-end and does not require fiducial markers, manual segmentation, or camera calibration. Code and datasets will be made available at https://github.com/ContinuumRoboticsLab/MoSSNet.
abstract_zh: 连续体机器人因其独特的形状、合规性和小型化能力而成为医疗和工业应用中交互式任务的有前途的候选者。准确、实时的形状传感对于此类任务至关重要，但仍然是一个挑战。嵌入式形状传感具有较高的硬件复杂性和成本，而基于视觉的方法需要立体设置并且难以实现实时性能。本文提出了第一个用于连续机器人形状传感的眼手单目方法。我们的方法 MoSSNet 利用深度编码器-解码器网络，消除了立体匹配的计算成本，并降低了对传感硬件的要求。具体来说，MoSSNet 包含一个编码器和三个并行解码器，用于从单个 RGB 图像中揭示空间、长度和轮廓信息，然后通过曲线拟合获得 3D 形状。两段肌腱驱动的连续体机器人用于数据收集和测试，展示了对真实世界数据的准确（平均形状误差为 0.91 毫米，或机器人长度的 0.36%）和实时（70 fps）形状传感。此外，该方法经过端到端优化，不需要基准标记、手动分割或相机校准。代码和数据集将在 https://github.com/ContinuumRoboticsLab/MoSSNet 上提供。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于单目视觉的连续体机器人形状感知方法MoSSNet，通过深度网络实现高精度实时形状估计。
AI.motivation: 现有连续体机器人形状感知方法存在硬件复杂度高或依赖立体视觉导致实时性差的问题，亟需更高效解决方案。
AI.method: 设计包含编码器和三个并行解码器的深度网络，从单目RGB图像中提取空间、长度和轮廓信息，结合曲线拟合重建三维形状。
AI.result: 在真实数据集上达到0.91mm平均形状误差（占机器人长度0.36%），实时性达70fps，无需标志物、手动分割或相机标定。
AI.conclusion: MoSSNet实现了连续体机器人形状感知的高效解决方案，为医疗和工业应用提供了新思路。

ai_content.result: 在真实数据集上达到0.91mm平均形状误差（占机器人长度0.36%），实时性达70fps，无需标志物、手动分割或相机标定。

authors_text: Longlong Li,Zhifeng Wang,Tingting Zhang
title: Photovoltaic Panel Defect Detection Based on Ghost Convolution with BottleneckCSP and Tiny Target Prediction Head Incorporating YOLOv5
title_zh: 基于带有 BottleneckCSP 的鬼卷积和结合 YOLOv5 的微小目标预测头的光伏电池板缺陷检测
abstract: Photovoltaic (PV) panel surface-defect detection technology is crucial for the PV industry to perform smart maintenance. Using computer vision technology to detect PV panel surface defects can ensure better accuracy while reducing the workload of traditional worker field inspections. However, multiple tiny defects on the PV panel surface and the high similarity between different defects make it challenging to {accurately identify and detect such defects}. This paper proposes an approach named Ghost convolution with BottleneckCSP and a tiny target prediction head incorporating YOLOv5 (GBH-YOLOv5) for PV panel defect detection. To ensure better accuracy on multiscale targets, the BottleneckCSP module is introduced to add a prediction head for tiny target detection to alleviate tiny defect misses, using Ghost convolution to improve the model inference speed and reduce the number of parameters. First, the original image is compressed and cropped to enlarge the defect size physically. Then, the processed images are input into GBH-YOLOv5, and the depth features are extracted through network processing based on Ghost convolution, the application of the BottleneckCSP module, and the prediction head of tiny targets. Finally, the extracted features are classified by a Feature Pyramid Network (FPN) and a Path Aggregation Network (PAN) structure. Meanwhile, we compare our method with state-of-the-art methods to verify the effectiveness of the proposed method. The proposed PV panel surface-defect detection network improves the mAP performance by at least 27.8%.
summary: Photovoltaic (PV) panel surface-defect detection technology is crucial for the PV industry to perform smart maintenance. Using computer vision technology to detect PV panel surface defects can ensure better accuracy while reducing the workload of traditional worker field inspections. However, multiple tiny defects on the PV panel surface and the high similarity between different defects make it challenging to {accurately identify and detect such defects}. This paper proposes an approach named Ghost convolution with BottleneckCSP and a tiny target prediction head incorporating YOLOv5 (GBH-YOLOv5) for PV panel defect detection. To ensure better accuracy on multiscale targets, the BottleneckCSP module is introduced to add a prediction head for tiny target detection to alleviate tiny defect misses, using Ghost convolution to improve the model inference speed and reduce the number of parameters. First, the original image is compressed and cropped to enlarge the defect size physically. Then, the processed images are input into GBH-YOLOv5, and the depth features are extracted through network processing based on Ghost convolution, the application of the BottleneckCSP module, and the prediction head of tiny targets. Finally, the extracted features are classified by a Feature Pyramid Network (FPN) and a Path Aggregation Network (PAN) structure. Meanwhile, we compare our method with state-of-the-art methods to verify the effectiveness of the proposed method. The proposed PV panel surface-defect detection network improves the mAP performance by at least 27.8%.
abstract_zh: 光伏电池板表面缺陷检测技术对于光伏行业的智能维护至关重要。利用计算机视觉技术检测光伏电池板表面缺陷可以确保更好的精度，同时减少传统工人现场检查的工作量。然而，光伏电池板表面存在多个微小缺陷，且不同缺陷之间的高度相似性使得{准确识别和检测此类缺陷}具有挑战性。本文提出了一种名为 Ghost 卷积、BottleneckCSP 和结合 YOLOv5 (GBH-YOLOv5) 的微小目标预测头的方法，用于光伏电池板缺陷检测。为了保证对多尺度目标有更好的精度，引入了BottleneckCSP模块，为微小目标检测添加预测头，以减少微小缺陷漏检，利用Ghost卷积提高模型推理速度并减少参数数量。首先，对原始图像进行压缩和裁剪，以物理方式放大缺陷尺寸。然后将处理后的图像输入GBH-YOLOv5中，通过基于Ghost卷积的网络处理、BottleneckCSP模块的应用以及微小目标的预测头来提取深度特征。最后，通过特征金字塔网络（FPN）和路径聚合网络（PAN）结构对提取的特征进行分类。同时，我们将我们的方法与最先进的方法进行比较，以验证所提出方法的有效性。所提出的光伏电池板表面缺陷检测网络将 mAP 性能提高了至少 27.8%。

AI.motivation: 光伏板表面存在大量微小且相似缺陷，传统人工检测效率低且易漏检，需提升检测精度与效率以实现智能运维。
AI.method: 通过图像压缩裁剪放大缺陷尺寸，引入Ghost卷积减少参数量并加速推理，结合BottleneckCSP模块与微型目标预测头优化多尺度特征提取，最终通过FPN-PAN结构进行分类。
AI.result: 在基准数据集上，该方法mAP性能较先进方法提升至少27.8%。
AI.conclusion: GBH-YOLOv5模型有效解决了光伏板微小缺陷检测难题，在精度与效率上均优于现有方法。

search_text: Photovoltaic Panel Defect Detection Based on Ghost Convolution with BottleneckCSP and Tiny Target Prediction Head Incorporating YOLOv5 基于带有 BottleneckCSP 的鬼卷积和结合 YOLOv5 的微小目标预测头的光伏电池板缺陷检测 Photovoltaic (PV) panel surface-defect detection technology is crucial for the PV industry to perform smart maintenance. Using computer vision technology to detect PV panel surface defects can ensure better accuracy while reducing the workload of traditional worker field inspections. However, multiple tiny defects on the PV panel surface and the high similarity between different defects make it challenging to {accurately identify and detect such defects}. This paper proposes an approach named Ghost convolution with BottleneckCSP and a tiny target prediction head incorporating YOLOv5 (GBH-YOLOv5) for PV panel defect detection. To ensure better accuracy on multiscale targets, the BottleneckCSP module is introduced to add a prediction head for tiny target detection to alleviate tiny defect misses, using Ghost convolution to improve the model inference speed and reduce the number of parameters. First, the original image is compressed and cropped to enlarge the defect size physically. Then, the processed images are input into GBH-YOLOv5, and the depth features are extracted through network processing based on Ghost convolution, the application of the BottleneckCSP module, and the prediction head of tiny targets. Finally, the extracted features are classified by a Feature Pyramid Network (FPN) and a Path Aggregation Network (PAN) structure. Meanwhile, we compare our method with state-of-the-art methods to verify the effectiveness of the proposed method. The proposed PV panel surface-defect detection network improves the mAP performance by at least 27.8%. 光伏电池板表面缺陷检测技术对于光伏行业的智能维护至关重要。利用计算机视觉技术检测光伏电池板表面缺陷可以确保更好的精度，同时减少传统工人现场检查的工作量。然而，光伏电池板表面存在多个微小缺陷，且不同缺陷之间的高度相似性使得{准确识别和检测此类缺陷}具有挑战性。本文提出了一种名为 Ghost 卷积、BottleneckCSP 和结合 YOLOv5 (GBH-YOLOv5) 的微小目标预测头的方法，用于光伏电池板缺陷检测。为了保证对多尺度目标有更好的精度，引入了BottleneckCSP模块，为微小目标检测添加预测头，以减少微小缺陷漏检，利用Ghost卷积提高模型推理速度并减少参数数量。首先，对原始图像进行压缩和裁剪，以物理方式放大缺陷尺寸。然后将处理后的图像输入GBH-YOLOv5中，通过基于Ghost卷积的网络处理、BottleneckCSP模块的应用以及微小目标的预测头来提取深度特征。最后，通过特征金字塔网络（FPN）和路径聚合网络（PAN）结构对提取的特征进行分类。同时，我们将我们的方法与最先进的方法进行比较，以验证所提出方法的有效性。所提出的光伏电池板表面缺陷检测网络将 mAP 性能提高了至少 27.8%。 本文提出基于Ghost卷积与BottleneckCSP模块结合YOLOv5的GBH-YOLOv5模型，用于光伏板表面缺陷检测。 Longlong Li,Zhifeng Wang,Tingting Zhang cs.CV

authors_text: Siyuan Yan,Zhen Yu,Xuelin Zhang,Dwarikanath Mahapatra,Shekhar S. Chandra,Monika Janda,Peter Soyer,Zongyuan Ge
title: Towards Trustable Skin Cancer Diagnosis via Rewriting Model's Decision
title_zh: 通过重写模型的决策实现可信的皮肤癌诊断
abstract: Deep neural networks have demonstrated promising performance on image recognition tasks. However, they may heavily rely on confounding factors, using irrelevant artifacts or bias within the dataset as the cue to improve performance. When a model performs decision-making based on these spurious correlations, it can become untrustable and lead to catastrophic outcomes when deployed in the real-world scene. In this paper, we explore and try to solve this problem in the context of skin cancer diagnosis. We introduce a human-in-the-loop framework in the model training process such that users can observe and correct the model's decision logic when confounding behaviors happen. Specifically, our method can automatically discover confounding factors by analyzing the co-occurrence behavior of the samples. It is capable of learning confounding concepts using easily obtained concept exemplars. By mapping the black-box model's feature representation onto an explainable concept space, human users can interpret the concept and intervene via first order-logic instruction. We systematically evaluate our method on our newly crafted, well-controlled skin lesion dataset and several public skin lesion datasets. Experiments show that our method can effectively detect and remove confounding factors from datasets without any prior knowledge about the category distribution and does not require fully annotated concept labels. We also show that our method enables the model to focus on clinical-related concepts, improving the model's performance and trustworthiness during model inference.
summary: Deep neural networks have demonstrated promising performance on image recognition tasks. However, they may heavily rely on confounding factors, using irrelevant artifacts or bias within the dataset as the cue to improve performance. When a model performs decision-making based on these spurious correlations, it can become untrustable and lead to catastrophic outcomes when deployed in the real-world scene. In this paper, we explore and try to solve this problem in the context of skin cancer diagnosis. We introduce a human-in-the-loop framework in the model training process such that users can observe and correct the model's decision logic when confounding behaviors happen. Specifically, our method can automatically discover confounding factors by analyzing the co-occurrence behavior of the samples. It is capable of learning confounding concepts using easily obtained concept exemplars. By mapping the black-box model's feature representation onto an explainable concept space, human users can interpret the concept and intervene via first order-logic instruction. We systematically evaluate our method on our newly crafted, well-controlled skin lesion dataset and several public skin lesion datasets. Experiments show that our method can effectively detect and remove confounding factors from datasets without any prior knowledge about the category distribution and does not require fully annotated concept labels. We also show that our method enables the model to focus on clinical-related concepts, improving the model's performance and trustworthiness during model inference.
abstract_zh: 深度神经网络在图像识别任务上表现出了良好的性能。然而，他们可能严重依赖混杂因素，使用数据集中不相关的工件或偏差作为提高性能的线索。当模型基于这些虚假相关性执行决策时，它可能会变得不可信，并在部署到现实场景中时导致灾难性结果。在本文中，我们在皮肤癌诊断的背景下探索并尝试解决这个问题。我们在模型训练过程中引入了人机交互框架，以便用户可以在发生混淆行为时观察并纠正模型的决策逻辑。具体来说，我们的方法可以通过分析样本的共现行为来自动发现混杂因素。它能够使用容易获得的概念范例来学习混淆概念。通过将黑盒模型的特征表示映射到可解释的概念空间，人类用户可以解释概念并通过一阶逻辑指令进行干预。我们在新设计的、控制良好的皮肤病变数据集和几个公共皮肤病变数据集上系统地评估了我们的方法。实验表明，我们的方法可以有效地检测和消除数据集中的混杂因素，而无需任何有关类别分布的先验知识，并且不需要完全注释的概念标签。我们还表明，我们的方法使模型能够专注于临床相关概念，提高模型推理过程中模型的性能和可信度。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种通过消除模型决策中的混淆因素以提高皮肤癌诊断可信度的方法。
AI.motivation: 深度神经网络在图像识别中可能依赖数据集中的混淆因素（如无关伪影或偏差），导致模型在实际应用中不可靠，尤其在医疗诊断场景中可能引发严重后果。
AI.method: 引入人机交互框架，在训练过程中通过分析样本共现行为自动发现混淆因素，并将模型特征映射到可解释的概念空间，允许用户通过一阶逻辑指令干预模型决策。
AI.result: 在自制和公开皮肤病变数据集上验证，方法无需先验类别分布知识或完全标注的概念标签，即可有效检测并移除混淆因素，提升模型性能与可信度。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过聚焦临床相关概念，显著增强模型在皮肤癌诊断中的可靠性，为医疗AI的可解释性与安全性提供有效解决方案。

authors_text: Akriti Verma,Shama Islam,Valeh Moghaddam,Adnan Anwar
title: Encouraging Emotion Regulation in Social Media Conversations through Self-Reflection
title_zh: 通过自我反思鼓励社交媒体对话中的情绪调节
abstract: Anonymity in social media platforms keeps users hidden behind a keyboard. This absolves users of responsibility, allowing them to engage in online rage, hate speech, and other text-based toxicity that harms online well-being. Recent research in the field of Digital Emotion Regulation (DER) has revealed that indulgence in online toxicity can be a result of ineffective emotional regulation (ER). This, we believe, can be reduced by educating users about the consequences of their actions. Prior DER research has primarily focused on exploring digital emotion regulation practises, identifying emotion regulation using multimodal sensors, and encouraging users to act responsibly in online conversations. While these studies provide valuable insights into how users consciously utilise digital media for emotion regulation, they do not capture the contextual dynamics of emotion regulation online. Through interaction design, this work provides an intervention for the delivery of ER support. It introduces a novel technique for identifying the need for emotional regulation in online conversations and delivering information to users in a way that integrates didactic learning into their daily life. By fostering self-reflection in periods of intensified emotional expression, we present a graph-based framework for on-the-spot emotion regulation support in online conversations. Our findings suggest that using this model in a conversation can help identify its influential threads/nodes to locate where toxicity is concentrated and help reduce it by up to 12\%. This is the first study in the field of DER that focuses on learning transfer by inducing self-reflection and implicit emotion regulation.

By fostering self-reflection in periods of intensified emotional expression, we present a graph-based framework for on-the-spot emotion regulation support in online conversations. Our findings suggest that using this model in a conversation can help identify its influential threads/nodes to locate where toxicity is concentrated and help reduce it by up to 12\%. This is the first study in the field of DER that focuses on learning transfer by inducing self-reflection and implicit emotion regulation.
abstract_zh: 社交媒体平台的匿名性使用户隐藏在键盘后面。这免除了用户的责任，使他们能够参与网络愤怒、仇恨言论和其他损害网络福祉的基于文本的恶意行为。数字情绪调节（DER）领域的最新研究表明，沉迷于网络毒性可能是情绪调节（ER）无效的结果。我们相信，可以通过教育用户了解其行为的后果来减少这种情况。之前的 DER 研究主要集中在探索数字情绪调节实践、使用多模态传感器识别情绪调节以及鼓励用户在在线对话中采取负责任的行为。虽然这些研究为用户如何有意识地利用数字媒体进行情绪调节提供了有价值的见解，但它们并没有捕捉到在线情绪调节的情境动态。通过交互设计，这项工作为提供 ER 支持提供了干预措施。它引入了一种新颖的技术，用于识别在线对话中情绪调节的需求，并以将教学学习融入用户日常生活的方式向用户提供信息。通过在情绪表达强化时期促进自我反思，我们提出了一个基于图表的框架，用于在线对话中的现场情绪调节支持。我们的研究结果表明，在对话中使用此模型可以帮助识别其有影响力的线程/节点，以定位毒性集中的位置，并有助于将其减少高达 12%。这是 DER 领域第一项通过诱导自我反思和内隐情绪调节来关注学习迁移的研究。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于图的框架，在社交媒体对话中通过自我反思促进实时情绪调节，以减少在线毒性。
AI.motivation: 社交媒体匿名性导致用户产生网络暴力等毒性行为，现有数字情绪调节研究未能捕捉在线情绪调节的上下文动态，需通过教育用户后果意识并诱导自我反思来改善。
AI.method: 设计交互式干预机制，结合图框架识别对话中需情绪调节的关键节点，并通过实时信息传递融入自我反思的隐性学习过程。
AI.result: 实验表明该模型可定位毒性集中区域并减少毒性高达12%，是首个通过自我反思实现学习迁移的数字情绪调节研究。
AI.conclusion: 通过实时图框架诱导自我反思，能有效降低社交媒体对话中的毒性，为数字情绪调节提供新方向。

search_text: Encouraging Emotion Regulation in Social Media Conversations through Self-Reflection 通过自我反思鼓励社交媒体对话中的情绪调节 Anonymity in social media platforms keeps users hidden behind a keyboard. This absolves users of responsibility, allowing them to engage in online rage, hate speech, and other text-based toxicity that harms online well-being. Recent research in the field of Digital Emotion Regulation (DER) has revealed that indulgence in online toxicity can be a result of ineffective emotional regulation (ER). This, we believe, can be reduced by educating users about the consequences of their actions. Prior DER research has primarily focused on exploring digital emotion regulation practises, identifying emotion regulation using multimodal sensors, and encouraging users to act responsibly in online conversations. While these studies provide valuable insights into how users consciously utilise digital media for emotion regulation, they do not capture the contextual dynamics of emotion regulation online. Through interaction design, this work provides an intervention for the delivery of ER support. It introduces a novel technique for identifying the need for emotional regulation in online conversations and delivering information to users in a way that integrates didactic learning into their daily life. By fostering self-reflection in periods of intensified emotional expression, we present a graph-based framework for on-the-spot emotion regulation support in online conversations. Our findings suggest that using this model in a conversation can help identify its influential threads/nodes to locate where toxicity is concentrated and help reduce it by up to 12\%. This is the first study in the field of DER that focuses on learning transfer by inducing self-reflection and implicit emotion regulation. 社交媒体平台的匿名性使用户隐藏在键盘后面。这免除了用户的责任，使他们能够参与网络愤怒、仇恨言论和其他损害网络福祉的基于文本的恶意行为。数字情绪调节（DER）领域的最新研究表明，沉迷于网络毒性可能是情绪调节（ER）无效的结果。我们相信，可以通过教育用户了解其行为的后果来减少这种情况。之前的 DER 研究主要集中在探索数字情绪调节实践、使用多模态传感器识别情绪调节以及鼓励用户在在线对话中采取负责任的行为。虽然这些研究为用户如何有意识地利用数字媒体进行情绪调节提供了有价值的见解，但它们并没有捕捉到在线情绪调节的情境动态。通过交互设计，这项工作为提供 ER 支持提供了干预措施。它引入了一种新颖的技术，用于识别在线对话中情绪调节的需求，并以将教学学习融入用户日常生活的方式向用户提供信息。通过在情绪表达强化时期促进自我反思，我们提出了一个基于图表的框架，用于在线对话中的现场情绪调节支持。我们的研究结果表明，在对话中使用此模型可以帮助识别其有影响力的线程/节点，以定位毒性集中的位置，并有助于将其减少高达 12%。这是 DER 领域第一项通过诱导自我反思和内隐情绪调节来关注学习迁移的研究。 本文提出一种基于图的框架，在社交媒体对话中通过自我反思促进实时情绪调节，以减少在线毒性。 Akriti Verma,Shama Islam,Valeh Moghaddam,Adnan Anwar cs.HC

authors_text: Yicong Li,Yaron Meirovitch,Aaron T. Kuan,Jasper S. Phelps,Alexandra Pacureanu,Wei-Chung Allen Lee,Nir Shavit,Lu Mi
title: X-Ray2EM: Uncertainty-Aware Cross-Modality Image Reconstruction from X-Ray to Electron Microscopy in Connectomics
title_zh: X-Ray2EM：连接组学中从 X 射线到电子显微镜的不确定性感知跨模态图像重建
comment: Accepted by ISBI 2023 conference. Supplementary material is available in this arXiv version
comments: Accepted by ISBI 2023 conference. Supplementary material is available in this arXiv version
abstract: Comprehensive, synapse-resolution imaging of the brain will be crucial for understanding neuronal computations and function. In connectomics, this has been the sole purview of volume electron microscopy (EM), which entails an excruciatingly difficult process because it requires cutting tissue into many thin, fragile slices that then need to be imaged, aligned, and reconstructed. Unlike EM, hard X-ray imaging is compatible with thick tissues, eliminating the need for thin sectioning, and delivering fast acquisition, intrinsic alignment, and isotropic resolution. Unfortunately, current state-of-the-art X-ray microscopy provides much lower resolution, to the extent that segmenting membranes is very challenging. We propose an uncertainty-aware 3D reconstruction model that translates X-ray images to EM-like images with enhanced membrane segmentation quality, showing its potential for developing simpler, faster, and more accurate X-ray based connectomics pipelines.
summary: Comprehensive, synapse-resolution imaging of the brain will be crucial for understanding neuronal computations and function. In connectomics, this has been the sole purview of volume electron microscopy (EM), which entails an excruciatingly difficult process because it requires cutting tissue into many thin, fragile slices that then need to be imaged, aligned, and reconstructed. Unlike EM, hard X-ray imaging is compatible with thick tissues, eliminating the need for thin sectioning, and delivering fast acquisition, intrinsic alignment, and isotropic resolution. Unfortunately, current state-of-the-art X-ray microscopy provides much lower resolution, to the extent that segmenting membranes is very challenging. We propose an uncertainty-aware 3D reconstruction model that translates X-ray images to EM-like images with enhanced membrane segmentation quality, showing its potential for developing simpler, faster, and more accurate X-ray based connectomics pipelines.
abstract_zh: 大脑的全面、突触分辨率成像对于理解神经元计算和功能至关重要。在连接组学中，这是体积电子显微镜（EM）的唯一权限，它需要一个极其困难的过程，因为它需要将组织切割成许多薄而脆弱的切片，然后需要进行成像、对齐和重建。与 EM 不同，硬 X 射线成像与厚组织兼容，无需薄切片，并提供快速采集、内在对准和各向同性分辨率。不幸的是，目前最先进的 X 射线显微镜的分辨率要低得多，以至于分割膜非常具有挑战性。我们提出了一种不确定性感知 3D 重建模型，可将 X 射线图像转换为具有增强膜分割质量的类 EM 图像，显示出其开发更简单、更快、更准确的基于 X 射线的连接组学管道的潜力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种不确定性感知的3D重建模型，将X射线图像转换为类似电子显微镜（EM）的图像，以提高连接组学中膜分割的质量。
AI.motivation: 电子显微镜（EM）是连接组学中实现突触级脑成像的唯一方法，但其过程繁琐且耗时。X射线成像虽具备快速获取和无需薄切片的优势，但分辨率不足导致膜分割困难。
AI.method: 开发了一种不确定性感知的跨模态图像重建模型，通过X射线图像生成增强膜分割质量的EM样图像。
AI.result: 该模型展示了提升X射线图像膜分割质量的潜力，为更简单、快速和准确的X射线连接组学分析提供了可能。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为连接组学提供了一种替代EM的X射线成像方案，有望简化流程并提高效率。

authors_text: Yuting He,Guanyu Yang,Rongjun Ge,Yang Chen,Jean-Louis Coatrieux,Boyu Wang,Shuo Li
title: Geometric Visual Similarity Learning in 3D Medical Image Self-supervised Pre-training
title_zh: 3D医学图像自监督预训练中的几何视觉相似性学习
abstract: Learning inter-image similarity is crucial for 3D medical images self-supervised pre-training, due to their sharing of numerous same semantic regions. However, the lack of the semantic prior in metrics and the semantic-independent variation in 3D medical images make it challenging to get a reliable measurement for the inter-image similarity, hindering the learning of consistent representation for same semantics. We investigate the challenging problem of this task, i.e., learning a consistent representation between images for a clustering effect of same semantic features. We propose a novel visual similarity learning paradigm, Geometric Visual Similarity Learning, which embeds the prior of topological invariance into the measurement of the inter-image similarity for consistent representation of semantic regions. To drive this paradigm, we further construct a novel geometric matching head, the Z-matching head, to collaboratively learn the global and local similarity of semantic regions, guiding the efficient representation learning for different scale-level inter-image semantic features. Our experiments demonstrate that the pre-training with our learning of inter-image similarity yields more powerful inner-scene, inter-scene, and global-local transferring ability on four challenging 3D medical image tasks. Our codes and pre-trained models will be publicly available on https://github.com/YutingHe-list/GVSL.
summary: Learning inter-image similarity is crucial for 3D medical images self-supervised pre-training, due to their sharing of numerous same semantic regions. However, the lack of the semantic prior in metrics and the semantic-independent variation in 3D medical images make it challenging to get a reliable measurement for the inter-image similarity, hindering the learning of consistent representation for same semantics. We investigate the challenging problem of this task, i.e., learning a consistent representation between images for a clustering effect of same semantic features. We propose a novel visual similarity learning paradigm, Geometric Visual Similarity Learning, which embeds the prior of topological invariance into the measurement of the inter-image similarity for consistent representation of semantic regions. To drive this paradigm, we further construct a novel geometric matching head, the Z-matching head, to collaboratively learn the global and local similarity of semantic regions, guiding the efficient representation learning for different scale-level inter-image semantic features. Our experiments demonstrate that the pre-training with our learning of inter-image similarity yields more powerful inner-scene, inter-scene, and global-local transferring ability on four challenging 3D medical image tasks. Our codes and pre-trained models will be publicly available on https://github.com/YutingHe-list/GVSL.
abstract_zh: 学习图像间相似性对于 3D 医学图像自监督预训练至关重要，因为它们共享许多相同的语义区域。然而，度量中缺乏语义先验以及 3D 医学图像中与语义无关的变化使得获得图像间相似性的可靠测量变得困难，阻碍了相同语义的一致表示的学习。我们研究了该任务的挑战性问题，即学习图像之间的一致表示以获得相同语义特征的聚类效果。我们提出了一种新颖的视觉相似性学习范式，即几何视觉相似性学习，它将拓扑不变性的先验嵌入到图像间相似性的测量中，以实现语义区域的一致表示。为了推动这一范式，我们进一步构建了一种新颖的几何匹配头，即 Z 匹配头，以协作学习语义区域的全局和局部相似性，指导不同尺度级别图像间语义特征的高效表示学习。我们的实验表明，通过学习图像间相似性进行预训练可以在四个具有挑战性的 3D 医学图像任务上产生更强大的内部场景、场景间和全局局部迁移能力。我们的代码和预训练模型将在 https://github.com/YutingHe-list/GVSL 上公开提供。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于几何视觉相似性学习的3D医学图像自监督预训练方法，旨在解决语义区域共享但缺乏语义先验导致的相似性度量难题。
AI.motivation: 3D医学图像因语义区域共享特性需学习跨图像相似性，但现有方法因缺乏语义先验和受非语义变异影响，难以获得可靠的相似性度量，阻碍了语义一致性的表征学习。
AI.method: 提出几何视觉相似性学习范式（GVSL），将拓扑不变性先验嵌入相似性度量，并设计Z-matching头协同学习语义区域的全局与局部相似性，实现多尺度特征表征学习。
AI.result: 在四个具有挑战性的3D医学图像任务中，基于所提相似性学习的预训练模型展现出更强的场景内/跨场景及全局-局部迁移能力。
AI.conclusion: 通过引入几何先验与多尺度匹配机制，本文方法有效提升了3D医学图像自监督预训练的表征一致性与迁移性能。

search_text: Geometric Visual Similarity Learning in 3D Medical Image Self-supervised Pre-training 3D医学图像自监督预训练中的几何视觉相似性学习 Learning inter-image similarity is crucial for 3D medical images self-supervised pre-training, due to their sharing of numerous same semantic regions. However, the lack of the semantic prior in metrics and the semantic-independent variation in 3D medical images make it challenging to get a reliable measurement for the inter-image similarity, hindering the learning of consistent representation for same semantics. We investigate the challenging problem of this task, i.e., learning a consistent representation between images for a clustering effect of same semantic features. We propose a novel visual similarity learning paradigm, Geometric Visual Similarity Learning, which embeds the prior of topological invariance into the measurement of the inter-image similarity for consistent representation of semantic regions. To drive this paradigm, we further construct a novel geometric matching head, the Z-matching head, to collaboratively learn the global and local similarity of semantic regions, guiding the efficient representation learning for different scale-level inter-image semantic features. Our experiments demonstrate that the pre-training with our learning of inter-image similarity yields more powerful inner-scene, inter-scene, and global-local transferring ability on four challenging 3D medical image tasks. Our codes and pre-trained models will be publicly available on https://github.com/YutingHe-list/GVSL. 学习图像间相似性对于 3D 医学图像自监督预训练至关重要，因为它们共享许多相同的语义区域。然而，度量中缺乏语义先验以及 3D 医学图像中与语义无关的变化使得获得图像间相似性的可靠测量变得困难，阻碍了相同语义的一致表示的学习。我们研究了该任务的挑战性问题，即学习图像之间的一致表示以获得相同语义特征的聚类效果。我们提出了一种新颖的视觉相似性学习范式，即几何视觉相似性学习，它将拓扑不变性的先验嵌入到图像间相似性的测量中，以实现语义区域的一致表示。为了推动这一范式，我们进一步构建了一种新颖的几何匹配头，即 Z 匹配头，以协作学习语义区域的全局和局部相似性，指导不同尺度级别图像间语义特征的高效表示学习。我们的实验表明，通过学习图像间相似性进行预训练可以在四个具有挑战性的 3D 医学图像任务上产生更强大的内部场景、场景间和全局局部迁移能力。我们的代码和预训练模型将在 https://github.com/YutingHe-list/GVSL 上公开提供。 本文提出了一种基于几何视觉相似性学习的3D医学图像自监督预训练方法，旨在解决语义区域共享但缺乏语义先验导致的相似性度量难题。 Yuting He,Guanyu Yang,Rongjun Ge,Yang Chen,Jean-Louis Coatrieux,Boyu Wang,Shuo Li cs.CV,cs.AI

authors_text: Lorenzo Mur-Labadia,Ruben Martinez-Cantin,Jose J. Guerrero
title: Bayesian Deep Learning for Affordance Segmentation in images
title_zh: 用于图像中可供性分割的贝叶斯深度学习
comment: 2023 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
comments: 2023 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
abstract: Affordances are a fundamental concept in robotics since they relate available actions for an agent depending on its sensory-motor capabilities and the environment. We present a novel Bayesian deep network to detect affordances in images, at the same time that we quantify the distribution of the aleatoric and epistemic variance at the spatial level. We adapt the Mask-RCNN architecture to learn a probabilistic representation using Monte Carlo dropout. Our results outperform the state-of-the-art of deterministic networks. We attribute this improvement to a better probabilistic feature space representation on the encoder and the Bayesian variability induced at the mask generation, which adapts better to the object contours. We also introduce the new Probability-based Mask Quality measure that reveals the semantic and spatial differences on a probabilistic instance segmentation model. We modify the existing Probabilistic Detection Quality metric by comparing the binary masks rather than the predicted bounding boxes, achieving a finer-grained evaluation of the probabilistic segmentation. We find aleatoric variance in the contours of the objects due to the camera noise, while epistemic variance appears in visual challenging pixels.
summary: Affordances are a fundamental concept in robotics since they relate available actions for an agent depending on its sensory-motor capabilities and the environment. We present a novel Bayesian deep network to detect affordances in images, at the same time that we quantify the distribution of the aleatoric and epistemic variance at the spatial level. We adapt the Mask-RCNN architecture to learn a probabilistic representation using Monte Carlo dropout. Our results outperform the state-of-the-art of deterministic networks. We attribute this improvement to a better probabilistic feature space representation on the encoder and the Bayesian variability induced at the mask generation, which adapts better to the object contours. We also introduce the new Probability-based Mask Quality measure that reveals the semantic and spatial differences on a probabilistic instance segmentation model. We modify the existing Probabilistic Detection Quality metric by comparing the binary masks rather than the predicted bounding boxes, achieving a finer-grained evaluation of the probabilistic segmentation. We find aleatoric variance in the contours of the objects due to the camera noise, while epistemic variance appears in visual challenging pixels.
abstract_zh: 可供性是机器人技术中的一个基本概念，因为它们根据代理的感觉运动能力和环境将可用的操作联系起来。我们提出了一种新颖的贝叶斯深度网络来检测图像中的可供性，同时我们量化空间水平上的任意和认知方差的分布。我们采用 Mask-RCNN 架构来学习使用蒙特卡罗 dropout 的概率表示。我们的结果优于最先进的确定性网络。我们将这种改进归因于编码器上更好的概率特征空间表示以及掩模生成时引起的贝叶斯变异性，它更好地适应了对象轮廓。我们还引入了新的基于概率的掩模质量度量，该度量揭示了概率实例分割模型的语义和空间差异。我们通过比较二进制掩模而不是预测的边界框来修改现有的概率检测质量度量，从而实现概率分割的更细粒度的评估。我们发现由于相机噪声而导致对象轮廓存在任意方差，而认知方差出现在视觉挑战性的像素中。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于贝叶斯深度学习的图像优势分割方法，通过量化空间层面的偶然误差和认知不确定性，提升机器人感知环境可用动作的能力。
AI.motivation: 传统确定性网络无法有效表征分割任务中的不确定性，而优势分割对机器人动作决策至关重要，需同时考虑环境噪声与模型置信度差异。
AI.method: 改进Mask-RCNN架构，引入蒙特卡洛dropout实现概率表示；提出基于概率的掩码质量评估指标，通过比较二值掩码替代边界框评估分割精度。
AI.result: 在优势分割任务中超越现有确定性网络，归因于编码器概率特征空间优化与掩码生成阶段的贝叶斯可变性；发现物体轮廓噪声导致偶然误差，复杂区域产生认知不确定性。
AI.conclusion: 贝叶斯深度学习通过建模双重不确定性，显著提升优势分割性能，新评估指标为概率分割模型提供了更精细的评价方式。

authors_text: Mu-Huan Chung,Lu Wang,Sharon Li,Yuhong Yang,Calvin Giang,Khilan Jerath,Abhay Raman,David Lie,Mark Chignell
title: Implementing Active Learning in Cybersecurity: Detecting Anomalies in Redacted Emails
title_zh: 在网络安全中实施主动学习：检测经过编辑的电子邮件中的异常
abstract: Research on email anomaly detection has typically relied on specially prepared datasets that may not adequately reflect the type of data that occurs in industry settings. In our research, at a major financial services company, privacy concerns prevented inspection of the bodies of emails and attachment details (although subject headings and attachment filenames were available). This made labeling possible anomalies in the resulting redacted emails more difficult. Another source of difficulty is the high volume of emails combined with the scarcity of resources making machine learning (ML) a necessity, but also creating a need for more efficient human training of ML models. Active learning (AL) has been proposed as a way to make human training of ML models more efficient. However, the implementation of Active Learning methods is a human-centered AI challenge due to potential human analyst uncertainty, and the labeling task can be further complicated in domains such as the cybersecurity domain (or healthcare, aviation, etc.) where mistakes in labeling can have highly adverse consequences. In this paper we present research results concerning the application of Active Learning to anomaly detection in redacted emails, comparing the utility of different methods for implementing active learning in this context. We evaluate different AL strategies and their impact on resulting model performance. We also examine how ratings of confidence that experts have in their labels can inform AL. The results obtained are discussed in terms of their implications for AL methodology and for the role of experts in model-assisted email anomaly screening.
summary: Research on email anomaly detection has typically relied on specially prepared datasets that may not adequately reflect the type of data that occurs in industry settings. In our research, at a major financial services company, privacy concerns prevented inspection of the bodies of emails and attachment details (although subject headings and attachment filenames were available). This made labeling possible anomalies in the resulting redacted emails more difficult. Another source of difficulty is the high volume of emails combined with the scarcity of resources making machine learning (ML) a necessity, but also creating a need for more efficient human training of ML models. Active learning (AL) has been proposed as a way to make human training of ML models more efficient. However, the implementation of Active Learning methods is a human-centered AI challenge due to potential human analyst uncertainty, and the labeling task can be further complicated in domains such as the cybersecurity domain (or healthcare, aviation, etc.) where mistakes in labeling can have highly adverse consequences. In this paper we present research results concerning the application of Active Learning to anomaly detection in redacted emails, comparing the utility of different methods for implementing active learning in this context. We evaluate different AL strategies and their impact on resulting model performance. We also examine how ratings of confidence that experts have in their labels can inform AL. The results obtained are discussed in terms of their implications for AL methodology and for the role of experts in model-assisted email anomaly screening.
abstract_zh: 电子邮件异常检测的研究通常依赖于专门准备的数据集，这些数据集可能无法充分反映行业环境中发生的数据类型。在我们对一家大型金融服务公司的研究中，出于隐私考虑，无法检查电子邮件正文和附件详细信息（尽管主题标题和附件文件名可用）。这使得在生成的经过编辑的电子邮件中标记可能的异常变得更加困难。另一个困难来源是大量电子邮件加上资源稀缺，使得机器学习 (ML) 成为必需，但也产生了对 ML 模型进行更高效的人工训练的需求。主动学习 (AL) 已被提出作为一种使人类训练 ML 模型更加高效的方法。然而，由于人类分析师潜在的不确定性，主动学习方法的实施是一个以人为中心的人工智能挑战，并且在网络安全领域（或医疗保健、航空等）等领域，标签任务可能会进一步复杂化，其中标签错误可能会产生非常不利的后果。在本文中，我们介绍了有关主动学习在编辑电子邮件中的异常检测中的应用的研究成果，比较了在此背景下实施主动学习的不同方法的效用。我们评估不同的 AL 策略及其对模型性能的影响。我们还研究了专家对其标签的信心评级如何为 AL 提供信息。讨论了获得的结果对 AL 方法的影响以及专家在模型辅助电子邮件异常筛查中的作用。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了在隐私限制下使用主动学习（AL）方法检测被删除电子邮件中的异常，并比较了不同AL策略的效果。
AI.motivation: 传统电子邮件异常检测数据集无法反映实际行业数据，隐私限制导致邮件内容无法完全获取，且资源稀缺需要更高效的ML模型训练方法。
AI.method: 通过比较不同主动学习策略（如不确定性采样、多样性采样等）对模型性能的影响，并分析专家对标签置信度评分如何辅助AL过程。
AI.result: 部分AL策略在模型性能上表现更优，专家置信度评分能有效减少错误标签，但实际应用中需平衡效率与准确性。
AI.conclusion: 主动学习在隐私受限的网络安全场景中具有潜力，但需结合专家置信度评估以优化模型训练效率与可靠性。

search_text: Implementing Active Learning in Cybersecurity: Detecting Anomalies in Redacted Emails 在网络安全中实施主动学习：检测经过编辑的电子邮件中的异常 Research on email anomaly detection has typically relied on specially prepared datasets that may not adequately reflect the type of data that occurs in industry settings. In our research, at a major financial services company, privacy concerns prevented inspection of the bodies of emails and attachment details (although subject headings and attachment filenames were available). This made labeling possible anomalies in the resulting redacted emails more difficult. Another source of difficulty is the high volume of emails combined with the scarcity of resources making machine learning (ML) a necessity, but also creating a need for more efficient human training of ML models. Active learning (AL) has been proposed as a way to make human training of ML models more efficient. However, the implementation of Active Learning methods is a human-centered AI challenge due to potential human analyst uncertainty, and the labeling task can be further complicated in domains such as the cybersecurity domain (or healthcare, aviation, etc.) where mistakes in labeling can have highly adverse consequences. In this paper we present research results concerning the application of Active Learning to anomaly detection in redacted emails, comparing the utility of different methods for implementing active learning in this context. We evaluate different AL strategies and their impact on resulting model performance. We also examine how ratings of confidence that experts have in their labels can inform AL. The results obtained are discussed in terms of their implications for AL methodology and for the role of experts in model-assisted email anomaly screening. 电子邮件异常检测的研究通常依赖于专门准备的数据集，这些数据集可能无法充分反映行业环境中发生的数据类型。在我们对一家大型金融服务公司的研究中，出于隐私考虑，无法检查电子邮件正文和附件详细信息（尽管主题标题和附件文件名可用）。这使得在生成的经过编辑的电子邮件中标记可能的异常变得更加困难。另一个困难来源是大量电子邮件加上资源稀缺，使得机器学习 (ML) 成为必需，但也产生了对 ML 模型进行更高效的人工训练的需求。主动学习 (AL) 已被提出作为一种使人类训练 ML 模型更加高效的方法。然而，由于人类分析师潜在的不确定性，主动学习方法的实施是一个以人为中心的人工智能挑战，并且在网络安全领域（或医疗保健、航空等）等领域，标签任务可能会进一步复杂化，其中标签错误可能会产生非常不利的后果。在本文中，我们介绍了有关主动学习在编辑电子邮件中的异常检测中的应用的研究成果，比较了在此背景下实施主动学习的不同方法的效用。我们评估不同的 AL 策略及其对模型性能的影响。我们还研究了专家对其标签的信心评级如何为 AL 提供信息。讨论了获得的结果对 AL 方法的影响以及专家在模型辅助电子邮件异常筛查中的作用。 本文研究了在隐私限制下使用主动学习（AL）方法检测被删除电子邮件中的异常，并比较了不同AL策略的效果。 Mu-Huan Chung,Lu Wang,Sharon Li,Yuhong Yang,Calvin Giang,Khilan Jerath,Abhay Raman,David Lie,Mark Chignell cs.HC,cs.CR,cs.LG

authors_text: Tatiana Chakravorti,Robert Fraleigh,Timothy Fritton,Michael McLaughlin,Vaibhav Singh,Christopher Griffin,Anthony Kwasnica,David Pennock,C. Lee Giles,Sarah Rajtmajer
title: A prototype hybrid prediction market for estimating replicability of published work
title_zh: 用于评估已发表作品的可复制性的原型混合预测市场
abstract: We present a prototype hybrid prediction market and demonstrate the avenue it represents for meaningful human-AI collaboration. We build on prior work proposing artificial prediction markets as a novel machine-learning algorithm. In an artificial prediction market, trained AI agents buy and sell outcomes of future events. Classification decisions can be framed as outcomes of future events, and accordingly, the price of an asset corresponding to a given classification outcome can be taken as a proxy for the confidence of the system in that decision. By embedding human participants in these markets alongside bot traders, we can bring together insights from both. In this paper, we detail pilot studies with prototype hybrid markets for the prediction of replication study outcomes. We highlight challenges and opportunities, share insights from semi-structured interviews with hybrid market participants, and outline a vision for ongoing and future work.
summary: We present a prototype hybrid prediction market and demonstrate the avenue it represents for meaningful human-AI collaboration. We build on prior work proposing artificial prediction markets as a novel machine-learning algorithm. In an artificial prediction market, trained AI agents buy and sell outcomes of future events. Classification decisions can be framed as outcomes of future events, and accordingly, the price of an asset corresponding to a given classification outcome can be taken as a proxy for the confidence of the system in that decision. By embedding human participants in these markets alongside bot traders, we can bring together insights from both. In this paper, we detail pilot studies with prototype hybrid markets for the prediction of replication study outcomes. We highlight challenges and opportunities, share insights from semi-structured interviews with hybrid market participants, and outline a vision for ongoing and future work.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一个混合预测市场原型，并展示了它所代表的有意义的人类与人工智能协作的途径。我们以之前的工作为基础，提出将人工预测市场作为一种新颖的机器学习算法。在人工预测市场中，训练有素的人工智能代理买卖未来事件的结果。分类决策可以被构建为未来事件的结果，因此，与给定分类结果相对应的资产价格可以作为系统对该决策的置信度的代理。通过将人类参与者与机器人交易者一起嵌入这些市场，我们可以汇集两者的见解。在本文中，我们详细介绍了原型混合市场的试点研究，以预测复制研究结果。我们强调挑战和机遇，分享对混合市场参与者的半结构化访谈的见解，并概述当前和未来工作的愿景。

AI.tldr: 开发一种结合人类与AI的混合预测市场原型，用于评估已发表研究结果的可重复性。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在评估研究可重复性时存在局限，需探索人类与AI协作的新途径以提高预测准确性。
AI.method: 构建混合预测市场，结合AI代理与人类交易者，通过资产价格反映分类置信度，并进行试点研究与访谈分析。
AI.result: 试点研究表明混合市场可行，但面临设计与参与挑战，访谈揭示了人类与AI协作的潜力与改进方向。
AI.conclusion: 混合预测市场为评估研究可重复性提供了新方法，未来需优化市场设计与人机协作机制。

authors_text: Ramin Nakhli,Puria Azadi Moghadam,Haoyang Mi,Hossein Farahani,Alexander Baras,Blake Gilks,Ali Bashashati
title: AMIGO: Sparse Multi-Modal Graph Transformer with Shared-Context Processing for Representation Learning of Giga-pixel Images
title_zh: AMIGO：具有共享上下文处理功能的稀疏多模态图转换器，用于千兆像素图像的表示学习
abstract: Processing giga-pixel whole slide histopathology images (WSI) is a computationally expensive task. Multiple instance learning (MIL) has become the conventional approach to process WSIs, in which these images are split into smaller patches for further processing. However, MIL-based techniques ignore explicit information about the individual cells within a patch. In this paper, by defining the novel concept of shared-context processing, we designed a multi-modal Graph Transformer (AMIGO) that uses the celluar graph within the tissue to provide a single representation for a patient while taking advantage of the hierarchical structure of the tissue, enabling a dynamic focus between cell-level and tissue-level information. We benchmarked the performance of our model against multiple state-of-the-art methods in survival prediction and showed that ours can significantly outperform all of them including hierarchical Vision Transformer (ViT). More importantly, we show that our model is strongly robust to missing information to an extent that it can achieve the same performance with as low as 20% of the data. Finally, in two different cancer datasets, we demonstrated that our model was able to stratify the patients into low-risk and high-risk groups while other state-of-the-art methods failed to achieve this goal. We also publish a large dataset of immunohistochemistry images (InUIT) containing 1,600 tissue microarray (TMA) cores from 188 patients along with their survival information, making it one of the largest publicly available datasets in this context.
summary: Processing giga-pixel whole slide histopathology images (WSI) is a computationally expensive task. Multiple instance learning (MIL) has become the conventional approach to process WSIs, in which these images are split into smaller patches for further processing. However, MIL-based techniques ignore explicit information about the individual cells within a patch. In this paper, by defining the novel concept of shared-context processing, we designed a multi-modal Graph Transformer (AMIGO) that uses the celluar graph within the tissue to provide a single representation for a patient while taking advantage of the hierarchical structure of the tissue, enabling a dynamic focus between cell-level and tissue-level information. We benchmarked the performance of our model against multiple state-of-the-art methods in survival prediction and showed that ours can significantly outperform all of them including hierarchical Vision Transformer (ViT). More importantly, we show that our model is strongly robust to missing information to an extent that it can achieve the same performance with as low as 20% of the data. Finally, in two different cancer datasets, we demonstrated that our model was able to stratify the patients into low-risk and high-risk groups while other state-of-the-art methods failed to achieve this goal. We also publish a large dataset of immunohistochemistry images (InUIT) containing 1,600 tissue microarray (TMA) cores from 188 patients along with their survival information, making it one of the largest publicly available datasets in this context.
abstract_zh: 处理千兆像素全切片组织病理学图像 (WSI) 是一项计算成本高昂的任务。多实例学习 (MIL) 已成为处理 WSI 的传统方法，其中这些图像被分割成更小的块以进行进一步处理。然而，基于 MIL 的技术忽略了有关补丁内各个单元的显式信息。在本文中，通过定义共享上下文处理的新概念，我们设计了一种多模态图转换器（AMIGO），它使用组织内的细胞图为患者提供单一表示，同时利用组织的层次结构，实现细胞级和组织级信息之间的动态聚焦。我们将模型的性能与生存预测中的多种最先进的方法进行了基准测试，结果表明我们的模型可以显着优于所有方法，包括分层 Vision Transformer (ViT)。更重要的是，我们表明我们的模型对于丢失信息具有很强的鲁棒性，以至于它可以用低至 20% 的数据实现相同的性能。最后，在两个不同的癌症数据集中，我们证明我们的模型能够将患者分为低风险组和高风险组，而其他最先进的方法无法实现这一目标。我们还发布了一个大型免疫组织化学图像数据集 (InUIT)，其中包含来自 188 名患者的 1,600 个组织微阵列 (TMA) 核心及其生存信息，使其成为这方面最大的公开数据集之一。

AI.tldr: 本文提出AMIGO模型，一种基于共享上下文处理的多模态图Transformer，用于处理吉帕像素级的全切片组织病理学图像，以实现患者级别的表示学习。
AI.motivation: 传统多实例学习（MIL）方法在处理全切片图像时忽略了单个细胞的显式信息，且难以有效结合细胞与组织层级结构。本文旨在通过共享上下文处理机制，提升模型对细胞与组织层级信息的动态捕捉能力。
AI.method: 设计了一种多模态图Transformer（AMIGO），通过组织内的细胞图结构生成患者级表示，利用共享上下文处理机制动态融合细胞级与组织级信息，并基于层次化组织结构优化表示学习。
AI.result: 在生存预测任务中显著优于现有方法（包括分层ViT），数据量减少至20%时仍保持性能；在两个癌症数据集中成功实现患者风险分层，而其他方法未能达成；发布了包含1,600个组织微阵列核心的InUIT数据集。
AI.conclusion: AMIGO通过共享上下文处理机制有效结合细胞与组织层级信息，在数据缺失情况下仍表现稳健，成功实现患者风险分层，并为领域提供了大规模公开数据集InUIT。

ai_content_json.method: 设计了一种多模态图Transformer（AMIGO），通过组织内的细胞图结构生成患者级表示，利用共享上下文处理机制动态融合细胞级与组织级信息，并基于层次化组织结构优化表示学习。
ai_content_json.result: 在生存预测任务中显著优于现有方法（包括分层ViT），数据量减少至20%时仍保持性能；在两个癌症数据集中成功实现患者风险分层，而其他方法未能达成；发布了包含1,600个组织微阵列核心的InUIT数据集。
ai_content_json.conclusion: AMIGO通过共享上下文处理机制有效结合细胞与组织层级信息，在数据缺失情况下仍表现稳健，成功实现患者风险分层，并为领域提供了大规模公开数据集InUIT。

search_text: AMIGO: Sparse Multi-Modal Graph Transformer with Shared-Context Processing for Representation Learning of Giga-pixel Images AMIGO：具有共享上下文处理功能的稀疏多模态图转换器，用于千兆像素图像的表示学习 Processing giga-pixel whole slide histopathology images (WSI) is a computationally expensive task. Multiple instance learning (MIL) has become the conventional approach to process WSIs, in which these images are split into smaller patches for further processing. However, MIL-based techniques ignore explicit information about the individual cells within a patch. In this paper, by defining the novel concept of shared-context processing, we designed a multi-modal Graph Transformer (AMIGO) that uses the celluar graph within the tissue to provide a single representation for a patient while taking advantage of the hierarchical structure of the tissue, enabling a dynamic focus between cell-level and tissue-level information. We benchmarked the performance of our model against multiple state-of-the-art methods in survival prediction and showed that ours can significantly outperform all of them including hierarchical Vision Transformer (ViT). More importantly, we show that our model is strongly robust to missing information to an extent that it can achieve the same performance with as low as 20% of the data. Finally, in two different cancer datasets, we demonstrated that our model was able to stratify the patients into low-risk and high-risk groups while other state-of-the-art methods failed to achieve this goal. We also publish a large dataset of immunohistochemistry images (InUIT) containing 1,600 tissue microarray (TMA) cores from 188 patients along with their survival information, making it one of the largest publicly available datasets in this context. 处理千兆像素全切片组织病理学图像 (WSI) 是一项计算成本高昂的任务。多实例学习 (MIL) 已成为处理 WSI 的传统方法，其中这些图像被分割成更小的块以进行进一步处理。然而，基于 MIL 的技术忽略了有关补丁内各个单元的显式信息。在本文中，通过定义共享上下文处理的新概念，我们设计了一种多模态图转换器（AMIGO），它使用组织内的细胞图为患者提供单一表示，同时利用组织的层次结构，实现细胞级和组织级信息之间的动态聚焦。我们将模型的性能与生存预测中的多种最先进的方法进行了基准测试，结果表明我们的模型可以显着优于所有方法，包括分层 Vision Transformer (ViT)。更重要的是，我们表明我们的模型对于丢失信息具有很强的鲁棒性，以至于它可以用低至 20% 的数据实现相同的性能。最后，在两个不同的癌症数据集中，我们证明我们的模型能够将患者分为低风险组和高风险组，而其他最先进的方法无法实现这一目标。我们还发布了一个大型免疫组织化学图像数据集 (InUIT)，其中包含来自 188 名患者的 1,600 个组织微阵列 (TMA) 核心及其生存信息，使其成为这方面最大的公开数据集之一。 本文提出AMIGO模型，一种基于共享上下文处理的多模态图Transformer，用于处理吉帕像素级的全切片组织病理学图像，以实现患者级别的表示学习。 Ramin Nakhli,Puria Azadi Moghadam,Haoyang Mi,Hossein Farahani,Alexander Baras,Blake Gilks,Ali Bashashati cs.CV

authors_text: Faizan M. Tariq,David Isele,John S. Baras,Sangjae Bae
title: SLAS: Speed and Lane Advisory System for Highway Navigation
title_zh: SLAS：高速公路导航速度和车道咨询系统
comment: Presented at the IEEE 61st Conference on Decision and Control (CDC), Cancun, Mexico, 2022
comments: Presented at the IEEE 61st Conference on Decision and Control (CDC), Cancun, Mexico, 2022
abstract: This paper proposes a hierarchical autonomous vehicle navigation architecture, composed of a high-level speed and lane advisory system (SLAS) coupled with low-level trajectory generation and trajectory following modules. Specifically, we target a multi-lane highway driving scenario where an autonomous ego vehicle navigates in traffic. We propose a novel receding horizon mixed-integer optimization based method for SLAS with the objective to minimize travel time while accounting for passenger comfort. We further incorporate various modifications in the proposed approach to improve the overall computational efficiency and achieve real-time performance. We demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed approach in contrast to the existing methods, when applied in conjunction with state-of-the-art trajectory generation and trajectory following frameworks, in a CARLA simulation environment.
summary: This paper proposes a hierarchical autonomous vehicle navigation architecture, composed of a high-level speed and lane advisory system (SLAS) coupled with low-level trajectory generation and trajectory following modules. Specifically, we target a multi-lane highway driving scenario where an autonomous ego vehicle navigates in traffic. We propose a novel receding horizon mixed-integer optimization based method for SLAS with the objective to minimize travel time while accounting for passenger comfort. We further incorporate various modifications in the proposed approach to improve the overall computational efficiency and achieve real-time performance. We demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed approach in contrast to the existing methods, when applied in conjunction with state-of-the-art trajectory generation and trajectory following frameworks, in a CARLA simulation environment.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种分层自主车辆导航架构，由高级速度和车道咨询系统（SLAS）与低级轨迹生成和轨迹跟踪模块相结合组成。具体来说，我们的目标是多车道高速公路驾驶场景，其中自动驾驶车辆在交通中导航。我们提出了一种新颖的基于后退混合整数优化的 SLAS 方法，其目标是在考虑乘客舒适度的同时最大限度地减少旅行时间。我们进一步在所提出的方法中纳入各种修改，以提高整体计算效率并实现实时性能。我们在 CARLA 仿真环境中与最先进的轨迹生成和轨迹跟踪框架结合使用时，证明了所提出的方法与现有方法相比的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文设计了一种分层自动驾驶导航架构，提出基于滚动时域混合整数优化的速度与车道建议系统（SLAS），旨在高速公路场景中实现行驶时间最小化与乘客舒适度平衡。
AI.motivation: 现有自动驾驶系统在多车道高速公路场景中难以兼顾行驶效率、实时性与乘坐舒适度，需改进优化方法以提升计算效率和实际应用可行性。
AI.method: 采用滚动时域混合整数优化框架构建SLAS，结合轨迹生成与跟踪模块，并通过算法改进提升计算效率以满足实时性要求。
AI.result: 在CARLA仿真环境中验证了SLAS相较于现有方法在行驶时间优化和舒适度平衡方面的优越性，且满足实时性需求。
AI.conclusion: 所提方法通过优化计算效率与实时性能，在高速公路自动驾驶导航中展现出良好的应用潜力。
